title: Trust Trump? Iran’s Doubts Shadow Peace Talks.
summary: Iranian leaders fear being burned again by President Trump, who tore up a nuclear agreement reached during the Obama administration after lengthy negotiations.

title: U.S. Turns Up Pressure on Iraq to Distance Itself From Iran
summary: Washington is demanding that the Baghdad government dismantle Iran-backed Iraqi militias that have been attacking Americans and U.S. sites there recently.

title: Europe Wanted a Say on the Iran War, but It’s Still on the Sidelines
summary: A British-French plan to secure the Strait of Hormuz would give the continent a role. But Tehran and Washington are still calling the shots.

title: Southern Poverty Law Center Says It Is Under Investigation by Justice Dept.
summary: The Southern Poverty Law Center, which is best known for investigating hate groups, has been accused by Republicans of unfairly targeting conservative and Christian organizations.

title: Virginia Redistricting Referendum: What to Watch
summary: Republicans have built a small advantage from the gerrymandering clash so far, but Virginia voters could change that.

title: What to Know About Virginia’s Redistricting Fight
summary: The election on Tuesday is part of a coast-to-coast fight between Republicans and Democrats ahead of the midterms.

title: The Redistricting War Has Come to Virginia
summary: Voters in Virginia are deciding whether to adopt a new gerrymandered congressional map that benefits Democrats. Nick Corasaniti, a politics reporter, explains the role Virginia’s new map is playing in the nationwide redistricting battle and its potential implications for the midterm elections.

title: Florida Democrat Resigns From Congress 20 Minutes Before Expulsion Vote
summary: Sheila Cherfilus-McCormick, a Florida Democrat, had been charged with stealing $5 million in federal funds. The House Ethics Committee was to take up her case Tuesday.

title: A Year After U.S.A.I.D.’s Death, Fired Workers Find Few Jobs and Much Loss
summary: People have plowed through savings, cashed out retirement funds and moved in with relatives. Former U.S.A.I.D. workers estimate that less than half have found full-time work.

title: ‘Wagyu’ Used to Guarantee Quality Beef. What Are You Paying for Today?
summary: Behind the scenes, competing forces battle for the reputation of prestige meat.

title: A Wish List for John Ternus, the Man Replacing Tim Cook as Apple’s CEO
summary: John Ternus, an Apple veteran who runs hardware engineering, will take over an extraordinarily profitable company in need of new ideas.

title: Tim Cook Was Very, Very Good at Making Money
summary: If the Steve Jobs era was defined by technological innovation, the Tim Cook period was one of exceptional financial growth.

title: ‘Donnyland’? Ukraine Proposes Renaming Part of the Donbas in Trump’s Honor.
summary: The proposal reflects a global reality in which governments appeal to President Trump’s vanity to get American might on their side.

title: A.I. Is Eliminating Jobs on Wall Street
summary: “A.I. gives us places to go we haven’t gone,” said one bank’s chief executive.

title: Florida Inquiry Into ChatGPT’s Role in FSU Shooting Shifts to Criminal Investigation
summary: The investigation focuses on messages between the chatbot and the man accused of killing two people at Florida State University last year.

title: Imprisoned for 30 Years, He Wooed Her With Love Poems
summary: After a forced confession and a death sentence, a Kurdish poet spent 30 years in jail, where he discovered his voice. His literary champion, to everyone’s surprise, became his life partner.

summary: The man who killed one tourist at a Mexican pyramid and wounded several others had materials in his backpack tied to a 1999 U.S. attack, a possible reference to the Columbine High School massacre.

title: Where Are All These Meteors Coming From?
summary: The frequency of fireballs in our planet’s skies seemed to grow in recent months. NASA and other meteor experts can’t agree on what explains it.

summary: At a starry premiere at Lincoln Center, fashion industry figures reflected on just how much the original film got right.

title: Why Are Palantir and OpenAI Scared of Alex Bores?
summary: Alex Bores is trying to win a congressional seat to represent part of New York City. Here’s why A.I. executives are funding the effort to stop him.

title: My Child Died of Measles
summary: If more people had gotten the vaccine, she might still be here.

summary: We look at the changes in gas prices.

title: 2 Americans Killed in Mexico Crash Were C.I.A. Members
summary: The two Americans were killed on Sunday when their vehicle crashed while returning from a counter-cartel operation led by Mexico’s armed forces in the state of Chihuahua.

title: Ohio State Details Relationship that Led to Former President Walter Carter Jr.’s Resignation
summary: The university released a report that said the president, Walter Carter Jr., took trips with a podcaster and suggested that the university hire her.

title: E.P.A., Maryland Sue D.C. Utility Over Potomac River Sewage
summary: In separate lawsuits, environmental regulators said D.C. Water failed to prevent and contain a massive spill of raw sewage upstream of Washington.
